# bot — Buffer of Thoughts: template-based meta-reasoning

> Identifies the problem type, retrieves an appropriate thought template (structured reasoning pattern), instantiates it for the specific problem, then verifies the result. Efficient because it leverages pre-defined reasoning patterns rather than discovering them from scratch each time.

## Contents

- [Parameters](#parameters)

## Parameters {#parameters}

| key | type | required | description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `ctx.gen_tokens` | number | optional | Max tokens per instantiate / verify step (default 500) |
| `ctx.task` | string | **required** | Problem to solve (required) |
| `ctx.templates` | map of string to shape { name: string, pattern: string } | optional | Custom template_key → {name, pattern} map; defaults to built-in TEMPLATES |

fields.doc: Final answer extracted from the verification LLM output (falls back to full verification text)
fields.name: answer

fields.doc: True when verification did not emit ERRORS: NONE (or NO ERRORS) — i.e., errors were reported
fields.name: errors_found

fields.doc: LLM output from Step 2 (template applied to the specific task)
fields.name: instantiated_reasoning

fields.doc: Selected template key; 'analytical' is used as a fallback when parsing fails
fields.name: template_key

fields.doc: Display name of the selected template
fields.name: template_name

fields.doc: Reasoning steps of the selected template
fields.name: template_pattern

fields.doc: Full Step-3 verification text including ERRORS: and FINAL ANSWER: sections
fields.name: verification
